title: main.c: nested #if blocks make init_ok state transitions hard to follow
description:
Location: main.c:96-144

The standalone LoRa init block has nested conditionals:
- Outer: #if CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 && !CONFIG_LICHEN_L2
- Inner: runtime checks (ret < 0, eui64 == NULL)
- Innermost: #if CONFIG_LICHEN_IPV6

init_ok is set in two different places depending on CONFIG_LICHEN_IPV6, but the LED turnoff happens unconditionally after the block, not gated by init_ok.

What goes wrong: Reader must mentally track which preprocessor paths are active to understand when init_ok becomes true. The LED logic being outside the success path suggests LED should turn off even on failure - but the comment says 'Turn off busy LED' implying success.

Suggested fix: Extract the init sequence into a static helper returning int (0 on success). Clearer structure: ret = init_standalone_lora(); if (ret == 0) { LOG_INF(...); }. LED logic becomes explicit.
close_reason: Fixed: Extracted nested init logic into init_standalone_lora() helper with clear error returns. LED turnoff now unconditional after init with explicit comment.
